rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHRISTOPHER KYLE COATS
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Arnold's Motion for Summary Judgment or in the Alternative Summary Adjudication is denied. Only Count 1 of the Second Amended Petition remains as Count 2 relates to a party which has settled. Count 1 claims that Arnold has estate tangible property and the proceeds of the sale of 459 Corbett Avenue belonging to the estate. Arnold failed to shift the burden of proof to the estate as he failed to prove that either one or more elements of the cause of action cannot be established or that there is a complete defense to the action. Arnold concedes that he had an agreement with Coats to pay $249,000 for the property "after he [Coats] moved out"; that he did not pay the note for the property; that he "borrowed" $249,000; and that after Coats died he stated "there's a strong argument that I owe your nephew" $249,000. Arnold Declaration Exhibts F and M. Arnold claims that on at least two occasions in February or March of 2004, Coats said that Arnold "would never actually pay anything for the property" and that "he watned me to have the property" but there is no evidnce other than Arnold's statement to support this allegation. Arnold's summary of his statement given to Melbostad contradicts this when he states "there's a strong argument that I owe your nephew". Even if Arnold shifted the burden, the estate raises triable issues of fact that the debt is owing has has not been paid. Exhibits to Sofge's Declaraton. Although the estate has abandoned its claim of undue influence, there is no separate cause of action for undue influence to be adjudicated. Objection to transcribed statement of Arnold, overruled, statement against interest, Arnold introduces portions of his statement which allows introduction of remaining portion of statement. Objections to depostiion, overruled, some of the same sections offered by Arnold. Prevailing party to prepare order. Any party contesting the tentative must notify the court (415-551-3702) and opposing counsel no later than 5:00 p.m. 7/29/14.
